sentences.text: Thank you.
Morning!
Good morning, how are you?
Good, how are you doing?
Good!
Sorry, I couldn't find the unmute button for a second, and then I realized I was unmuted, so worked out.
All good.
I'm 7 minutes late, so I feel like I should be doing the apologizing.
No, no, you're good.
Well, great, great to see you again.
Yeah, you too!
How long have you been over at, Horning for?
I think, maybe this is my month mark right now?
Okay, wow.
Yeah.
Awesome, awesome.
I'm interested to learn just, like, a little bit about Horning as a company.
I don't know a ton about them, so….
Yeah, yeah, so we're a bit smaller, than Gates, where I was before, and primarily owner-managers, so we own most of our assets with just a few third-party.
But all throughout the DMV, another solid mix of conventional and affordable, too, and then also an array of asset classes as well.
Awesome.
And you just wanna… you want me to just, like, walk you through what's… what's new with the product, basically?
That'd be great.

Yeah, I think I'm pretty familiar, just, because I… I remember Martha pulling up, like, one of those, like, user things, and I, like, shot past everyone, so I'm pretty familiar with the dashboard itself.
Yeah.
As well as the capabilities, but I'm really interested in seeing, how we could apply it here.
I know we have some individuals I've talked to who have actually used DepartmentIQ in the past and loved it.
So really just kind of, like, yes to the capabilities, but also to the potential rollout process, as well as, obviously, terms and costs for property.
Okay, gotcha.
And I guess, tell me a little bit, what are current processes there?
… It's a great question.
There aren't a ton.
Still learning that.
We're kind of… we're kind of rolling it out as we speak.
So it's really based on a property-by-property basis.
Some properties have their own manual market surveys that they do, and then some have nothing.
So really, we're kind of starting from scratch here.
Okay, gotcha.
And I guess in terms of, like, when we get to the pricing conversation, are you thinking about utilizing this the same way that you were at Gates Hudson, where it's all the way down to on-site teams?
And I guess, follow-up question to that, how many properties, how many units are in the portfolio?
Yeah, I'd ideally love that.
I really want the site teams to be able to kind of look into it, dig around, too.
We have about 40 properties as a whole.
Okay.
check all the unit count for you, but I want to say maybe, like, 5,000 tubs, maybe?
Maybe 10?
I'll have to check.
It's crazy.
I just, Chad GPT that, I just got, like, the newest version, it says 5,000, so we can go with that, but I can give you a few brackets, when we get there, so….
Okay, perfect.
Cool.
And it was founded by Joe Horning in 1958, is that…?
ChatGPT's doing great, yeah, exactly.
Awesome.
And Larry.
Larry, too.
There you go.
Yes.
Okay, very cool.
Let me get into it, and I'll just give you, like, a quick overview.
And then I guess in terms of… you obviously know, like, the operational component of this, which is you can set it up for each property.
Those reports we probably don't have to spend a time on, but… How do you all think about growth?
Are you generally doing, like, acquisition activity?
Is it ground-up development?
Any of that planned that you know of over the course of the next year?
Not that I know of for the next year.
I know that we just recently took over, or purchased Plaza Towers in Hyattsville, which is the first one in a while, I think, so we are within that mindset.
I just don't know the actual schedule going on.
No worries at all.
Pull up my screen, and then we can talk logistics following.
Let's do, like, a little 10-minute refresher, but… Cool.
Three areas for navigation within the tool.
So, this My Properties section, this is primarily, I think, what you all were using at Gates Hudson.
Toward the end.
Just recently, we started to talk about this research and explore component, but, just know that those exist.
few UI updates, like, in the last month or so.
And I guess, in terms of, like, class of property that you all are dealing with there, you said there's an affordable component.
For the conventional side, like, BC class, or is it A class properties?
Affordable included, I'd actually say A to C.
Okay, awesome.

So this is just, like, an important distinguishment that we're making now, is that within IQ, because of the way that we work, where it's a public data model, there's gonna be some metrics that are just observed, like, they are what they are, given, like, class of property, units, things like rent, we're pulling directly off the property website.
And then there's some metrics here that are calculated.
So, ones like advertised occupancy, this is generally the data point that's called into question the most, just because of things like hidden units.

We do as much as we can with artificial intelligence to smooth those numbers out and unlock those hidden units, but, just note now that this is, like, an important distinguishment that we're making, that some of these metrics are, calculated metrics.
Okay.
Should look pretty similar, again, like, you know, you're in complete control as to who you're tracking, within each one of these comp sets.
Anything can be added or removed at your convenience.
We've added in some new filtering in some different areas, so, if you're trying to get to a specific comp set, you can kind of filter down to, characteristics that you'd be looking for.
Market Survey now has, Property class filters, build-to-run filters.
And then this unit style filters.
So, for those affordable, if you did sign them on AIQ, you would have the ability to look at just affordable units.
… I think what else is new here?
I don't know if you recall, but, right, you have your property level information, Bed type information, and then fees and deposits.
And then amenities in-unit and at the community level.
And then two options for export here.
So, PDF is the one that's, like, generally on-site teams are using that.
It's rolling up the data, what changed week over week?
What are your, like, most standard performance metrics, rent, concessions, occupancy?
Fees and deposits… amenity information, all pulls out, nice PDF formatted report.
This Excel report here will just pull out each of these tabs, data into Excel so you can actually work with them.
Great.
How am I doing so far?
Is this like, you're like, I've seen this before, or you want me to keep rocking through?
Yeah, no, that'd be great.
Okay, I won't be offended if you're like, I know, I use this every day.
Floor plan level reporting here.
So, you can select your trend timeframe, essentially to see, you know, like, historically, how has a particular floor plan been performing?
what percent of the unit mix does it make up?
So this really allows you to kind of key in on, like product, if you have a particular floor plan that… As rents are trending down, and, … it looks like, you know, ones in general are going up.
You're able to kind of filter into that exact product and see, okay, well, maybe smaller one-beds are doing really well, but larger one-beds in this market are underperforming.
So it gives you those insights here.
Same thing for, if you're just looking at generalized bed types.
Here's your unit level information, so this is always, like, the use case for me with this, in a tight market where there's a lot of supply, highly competitive.
Again, you can filter into, like, product in that market.
And you can actually look at which units have been leasing over the last 30 days.
And so, I usually use this report to look at leased units, descending from most recent to 30 days back.
And then, am I seeing any, common threads of those units?
So, do they have varying lease lengths?
Are they on a revenue management software?
what's their concession percentages?
Is there specific amenities at those units that might be moving them?
And that just gives you insights, too, right?
Like, in this market, this is Nashville, ton of supply, you have to have some form of concession on, for the most part.
So that's that, and then historicals… This is just your backward-looking trend data, so… How have rents been performing?
I've got this filtered out of two beds, but how are rents?
How are concessions?
What's available with my competitors?
I did 3 leases last week, how does that compare to the other properties in the market?
How does it compare month over month?
And then where are occupancies?
Great.
Cool.
Then you have this research component.
So, I don't know how much we went into this while you were at Gates, but effectively here you can search any property.
And it's gonna create that same set of reports that we just went through.
Right.
If you do any ground-up development there, you can create, like, a custom subject property.
Excuse me, … Again, we'll pull the same set of reports, all the same data that you're getting here, you get here.
Great.
This Explorer section is the newest one, so, this is your broader market dynamics.
What do rents look like?
How much availability there is right now in this market?
And this can be… Any, geographical boundary you want, so you have, like, your free hand tools here.
You can click into… Right, some markets here.
search by MSA, county, zip code, however you want to look at the data.
Similar performance metrics you have just in your general comp set, obviously a broader set of data here.
And then directly out of this tool, you can build those research reports.
As well.
Perfect.
Also have new supply, so what's being built, and when is it delivering?

Don't have anything that's gonna land in Chelsea in the next… couple of years, but if you go to broader Boston, here's what's in planning, here's what's currently being built, here's what's scheduled.
And then you have, like, a summary of that project here.
Great.
And last thing is, if you are building somewhere, you are acquiring somewhere, what are the demographics, what's employment, … Is income trending upward or down?
Is population trending up or down?
all that data from, ACS or census data here.
Perfect.
Okay, … anything I can go back over, or… looks probably how I did a month ago, I'm guessing.
Yeah, it looks very similar to a month ago, so it's a good refresher.
Awesome.
This area here, just so you're aware, this will be the quickest growing area within IQ.
Right.
These three reports will be rolled into a market summary.
And, there will be an additional, tab here that's a market comparison tool.
So I know you have a lot in Boston, or sorry, DC.

But if you wanted to track markets and, like, how much supply is being delivered, what do transactions look like, how many new units are hitting the market, how has rent trended over the last year, That tool will be introduced very shortly.
And then we contracted with a third-party data supplier that would be giving us a lot more information on this inventory.
Who owns it?
What was its last sale price?
What are migration patterns, etc, so….
Awesome.
Just so you're aware, if that's something that you guys are interested in in the future, that exists.
I definitely know that there are a few individuals that would like access to that, I think, so, yeah.
The idea is… Eventually, you compete with CoStar, right?
So we get a lot of requests of what to do with our data.
That product is coming out, and then we just released the revenue management software as well.
Oh, great, okay.
Yeah.
So yeah, in the, in the, like, in terms of cost in terms, annual contract, 30 day out for, change of management or property sold is no problem.
But annual is, like, firm, we have no flexibility on that anymore.
And then, there's… you're thinking about using this operationally, right?
Like, down to on-site?
Yeah.
Okay, so, that's a cost per unit, and in the 2,000 to 6,000, unit bracket, that is, 85 cents a unit.
Okay.
And I think Gates was on some weird per-property, and, like, we… I think there's, like, 3 or 4 companies that are left on that.
… We don't offer that to new clients, unfortunately.
Gotcha.
Okay, so I will do some calculation on that, and see how that shapes up for us.
In regards to properties that are… okay, so first off, does that apply for every single unit in the… in the portfolio, or would that just be, like, on a case-by-case basis if we wanted.
Yeah, if you… if you signed 2001 units, it would be at 85 cents, so you don't have to bring the full portfolio.
Yeah.
Okay, and then second, so there's no special pricing for affordable communities, correct?
Or affordable units?
There's… there's not.
Okay.
Yeah.
Got it.

But I would… what we can do, and like, because I've worked with you before, we can get some of those research seats included in there, and then what you could do is just create those, affordable… Communities in there.
Understood, got it.
If you wanted to, yeah, you know, don't do it for everything, like, you know.
Because we'll catch it, but, if you need to pull reports for them, like, now and again, it's okay to do in there.
Okay, gotcha.
And would the pricing differ if it were based on, having site managers in there versus not?
Yeah, there's options if you just want to go, like, user-focused, so the first bracket for that is, up to 5 users is $15,000 annually, and then up to 10 users is $25,000, and then up to… 50 users.
Sorry, up to 25 users.
Know what'd I say?
Up to 5 is $15,000, up to 10 is $25,000, and up to 25 is $50,000 annually.
Got it.
Yep.
Okay.
Gotcha, gotcha.
Okay, thank you.
And then just, like, as they're having these internal conversations and getting used to things there, if there's anything we can do to make it work, it can give you some flexibility.
I know we've worked with you before.
Gates had a great, like, early adopter rate, so I'll try and help you out as much as I can.
Just, let me know what everybody's thinking.
Thank you, yeah, I appreciate that.

I think next, I'll probably want to introduce you around to some more people on the team, primarily our VP, I don't know his title, but he works a lot with a lot of, like, the technology, and integrations that we have.
So I'd love to introduce you to him.
Let me see what his title is.
Sorry, can you tell I'm still fresh on the learning?
All good.
I am laughing.
Yeah.
Yeah, can't even find his signature, that's fine.
I'll give you introductions to him once that happens, and then also one of our regionals, too.
So, hate to do it to you, but would love to maybe set this up again in a demo way, so that they can learn, ask their questions, and maybe we can see if we can move forward on something.
Yeah, happy to do that.
Take the introduction, and we'll find a time that works, and can do the whole song and dance, and see how everybody feels about it.
Sounds great.
Awesome.
Oh, thanks so much, Matthew!
Yeah, good to catch up.
Yeah, you as well.
Okay, bye-bye.
Alright, bye.
